notes.text: Sent list of items to ask for from NW Biotrust: Age of patient at collection; Patient gende;r Patient ethnicity / race; Sample collection date; Anatomic site of sample collected Pathology report of sample collected; Surgical / procedure note for collection; Most recent oncology note prior to surgery; Staging (TNM); Pre-biopsy chemotherapy / immunotherapy exposure (including all prior lines of therapy and date ranges of therapy; including exposure to immunotherapies such as ipilimumab, nivolumab, pembrolizumab, atezolizumab) Past medical history (including history of autoimmune disease, prior malignancies)

notes.text: Comments: -We can transduce HSCs in vivo and restrict expression to specific T cell subsets using certain promoters, but we cannot directly transduce T cells. This can be done to express TCRs or CARs.
-The advantage of our approach is continuous expression of these T cells -We use a suicide system to arrest T cells.
We have tested this using Ganciclovir.  -There is enough space in our vector to express immunostimulatory cytokines (CD40, IL2, etc)
- We need humanized BLT mice (we have CD46 transgenic mice) for this and are not sure what mice models you have or if the microbes work here.
Maybe use HDAd to create "deletable" T cells
Induce microbe T-cells to produce cytokines?  Or even just alloreactive T cells to produce cytokine? (e.g. marilyn, matahari)
Use promoter readout to capture activated or microbe-specific T cells? secondary transfers?
Uses retinoic acid receptor (RARE) reporter - drive a4/b7 gut imprint. -
